strongestIdea: Use Brass Threshold Key Light as the default Otis production lighting family: warm brass key, ivory face hold, tiny deep navy internal separation, and restrained burgundy accent warmth. It preserves the approved soft Santa concierge threshold-keeper identity while staying readable over the preserved lobby palette.
uniquenessClaim: This lane converts dramatic lighting into a production-safe hierarchy and QA gate instead of a mood-only art direction. It specifically stress-tests 128px face readability, red-muddy burgundy bounce, deep navy coat collapse, rim haloing, and identity drift.
qualityRisks: No generated image files were produced in this lane, so lighting recommendations are prompt and QA artifacts rather than visually preflighted sources.
The parent rejection ledger still blocks production on native 4K-class source resolution and alpha/chroma requirements.
Deep navy back-rim lighting can create haloing or make the coat collapse into deep navy UI backgrounds.
Burgundy side bounce can make Otis's skin and beard red-muddy at mobile scale.
Dramatic lighting can reduce the approved warm human concierge identity if the face hold is not enforced across all outfits and poses.
fallbackReason: The requested GPT-5.5 fast mode with extra-high reasoning was not exposed in this session, so the lane was completed with the available Codex GPT-5 environment.
promotionBlockers: No lane preflight JSON because no images were generated or inspected.
Existing generated-source resolution and alpha/chroma blockers remain unresolved.
Coordinator must run real image generation, source preflight, 128px readability checks, and edge halo checks before promotion.
